content: Hierarchical agent-to-agent memory gating in multi-agent systems can repurpose memory-efficient Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) implementations to only store forward prediction error features for high-uncertainty interactions between neighboring agents, cutting collective memory usage by up to 70% while preserving the curiosity-driven exploration that drives emergent coordination. This approach reframes agent-agent interaction as a form of intrinsic curiosity signal rather than just an explicitly rewarded communication channel.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
trigger_topic: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
